title: Bound and checkpoint operator state
description: Give stateful operators TTLs and reliable checkpoints so state neither grows without limit nor is lost on restart.
body: Use when an operator keeps keyed state such as joins, dedup, or session aggregates. Do not let keyed state accumulate forever — unbounded state eventually exhausts memory or disk and stalls the job. Actions: set state TTL or explicit cleanup on inactive keys, checkpoint state to durable storage on an interval, and verify a restart restores the same state. Validation signals include a flat or bounded state-size metric over time and a restart that resumes from the last checkpoint with consistent results. Risk boundary: state that grows unbounded crashes the job under load; if state cannot be bounded by TTL, escalate to redesign the keying before it reaches production scale.
